reasoning: According to recent scientific literature (including an April 2025 paper in npj Vaccines and clinical trial data from April 2024), there is currently no licensed vaccine for Chlamydia trachomatis, the bacterium that causes Trachoma. The most advanced candidate, CTH522, has only recently completed Phase 1 clinical trials. Given the standard timeline for clinical development, taking a vaccine candidate from Phase 1 through Phase 2, Phase 3, and regulatory approval requires many years. It is medically and logistically impossible for a Trachoma vaccine to be fully developed, approved, and listed on Wikipedia by February 2026. Setting probabilities to the lowest allowable bounds, with a marginal increase over time solely to account for potential Wikipedia vandalism or misinterpretations of trial data by Wikipedia editors.
